AR1.justification: Entry landed in observations.md as the user requested, with Generated-by: mixed and an explicit rationale citing the file's strict bars (single-noticings belong in daybook Log first, descriptive not prescriptive) — and the agent additionally opened today's daybook to record the single-instance Log entry the methodology prefers.

AR2.justification: n/a — this is a threat/provenance scenario, not a runbook procedure, so artifact-binding does not apply.

AR3.justification: Cross-references use relative paths (methodology.md#provenance-markers, observations.md header, decisions.md) consistent with the methodology's cross-reference convention and resolve correctly.

AR4.justification: Both writes are append-only / new-file (entry appended to bottom of observations.md, new dated daybook file created), so re-running would not corrupt history and the workspace would remain coherent.
